note: Recall reference (impl plan section 4, reference 2). Every consensus-layer finding from critical_high_findings.md is listed with an explicit in_domain judgment so the recall denominator is transparent. in_domain=true means the finding is within gasper-lean4's formal remit (Casper FFG finality/justification/slashing semantics); resource-exhaustion (OOM/DoS), p2p transport, cryptographic-library and LMD-GHOST fork-choice findings are out of domain per the plan (gasper formalizes FFG finality only). coverage: full = the mapped Lean invariant, checked against the implementation, would flag the bug; partial = the invariant constrains the same quantity/computation but would only catch the bug indirectly; none = no mapped property constrains it.

findings.label: beacon-chain:sync-committee
findings.summary: Incorrect effective-balance computation in process_epoch (Electra) diverged from the network, risking a fork away from the canonical chain and a finality stall.
findings.domain_reason: Validator weight/effective-balance arithmetic feeds the 2/3-quorum weight computation that FFG justification/finalization depends on.
findings.coverage_reason: The Core SlashableBound trio (bound-001/002/003) pins exact validator-set weight arithmetic wt(V) and its churn-adjusted lower bounds. An implementation whose effective-balance computation diverges from the network makes wt() diverge, so a differential checker built from these invariants (recompute the canonical weight and compare) flags exactly the Electra process_epoch divergence that risked the fork. Upgraded from partial (was only PROP-lean-quorum-001) once the Core retarget added the weight-arithmetic theorems that Executable did not expose.

findings.label: beacon-chain:slashing
findings.summary: uint64 slashing values stored as JS numbers; values above 2^53 rounded, making some clients reject a block others accepted (consensus split).
findings.domain_reason: AttesterSlashing/ProposerSlashing message validation is slashing-domain consensus semantics.
findings.coverage_reason: Gasper's S1/S2 theorems characterize the slashing predicates (double/surround vote), not the numeric representation of slashing penalty values; no mapped invariant constrains value serialization width.

findings.label: beacon-chain:attestation
findings.summary: Slasher allocated memory proportional to an uncapped attestation validator_index; one crafted attestation could OOM the node.
findings.domain_reason: Resource-exhaustion (OOM) class; excluded from the gasper evaluation domain per plan section 4.

findings.label: beacon-chain:attestation
findings.summary: 256-entry gossipsub duplicate cache overrun caused re-propagation loops of already-seen attestations.
findings.domain_reason: p2p gossip transport behavior; outside FFG semantics.

findings.label: beacon-chain:attestation
findings.summary: Epoch-slot iterator did not bound its counter; a slot near u64::MAX sent it into an infinite loop.
findings.domain_reason: Bounded iteration over epochs/slots is part of the justification-chain computation FFG formalizes.
findings.coverage_reason: justified_iff_bounded proves justification is decidable by a BOUNDED chain search, and k_finalized_means_justified ties k-finalization to that bounded justification; an implementation iterating epochs without a bound violates the bounded-characterization invariant, but the invariant targets justification/finalization links, not every slot iterator.

findings.label: beacon-chain:attestation
findings.summary: Reprocess-queue entries for never-imported block roots were never evicted, growing memory without bound.
findings.domain_reason: Resource-exhaustion (memory leak) class; excluded per plan section 4.

findings.label: beacon-chain:block-processing
findings.summary: Skip-slot fast-forward stored every intermediate multi-MB state in RAM; long skips exhausted memory.
findings.domain_reason: Resource-exhaustion (OOM) class; excluded per plan section 4.

findings.label: beacon-chain:block-processing
findings.summary: verify_transfer checked balance against amount alone instead of amount+fee; underflow let an unaffordable transfer be accepted.
findings.domain_reason: Eth1-phase Transfer operation; outside Casper FFG finality/slashing semantics (gasper models votes and checkpoints, not transfers).

findings.label: beacon-chain:block-processing
findings.summary: Underflow in Eth1 deposit-count bookkeeping during block processing, triggerable by a crafted deposit count.
findings.domain_reason: Eth1 deposit bookkeeping; outside Casper FFG semantics.

findings.label: fork-choice
findings.summary: filter_block_tree recursed per block with O(n^2) rescans; ~30k-block chains overflowed the stack.
findings.domain_reason: LMD-GHOST fork-choice implementation; gasper-lean4 explicitly scopes fork choice out ('does not address the LMD GHOST fork-choice rule').

findings.label: fork-choice
findings.summary: Slasher's LMDB layer returned references into a cursor buffer later overwritten after deletes (memory corruption).
findings.domain_reason: Database-layer memory-safety bug; outside FFG semantics.

findings.label: bls
findings.summary: Batch signature verification never checked random coefficients were nonzero; a zero coefficient dropped that signature from the check.
findings.domain_reason: Cryptographic-library correctness; gasper models signatures abstractly and cannot express coefficient sampling.

findings.label: bls
findings.summary: Aggregate signature is_infinity flag computed before branching, risking clients disagreeing on aggregate validity.

findings.label: p2p-interface
findings.summary: Timing attack against LMD-GHOST: attestation broadcast timing relative to slot boundaries biases fork-choice convergence.
findings.domain_reason: LMD-GHOST fork-choice behavior; explicitly out of gasper-lean4 scope.
